codeReview.summary: The logs page implementation fully satisfies all five feature requirements. The page was converted to 'use client' with client-side Supabase fetching from message_logs. Error handling properly displays a user-visible 'Failed to load messages' error with a 'Try Again' button instead of an empty state. Auto-refresh polling at 10s intervals is implemented via useEffect + setInterval with a Live/Paused toggle and manual Refresh button. Inbound messages are styled blue (left-aligned) and outbound messages green (right-aligned), matching the spec. Messages load on initial mount via fetchMessages(true). The code follows the established dashboard-client.tsx pattern closely. TypeScript compilation and Next.js build both passed. Agent-browser verification confirmed the error state renders correctly (Supabase env not available in test env). Two non-blocking issues identified.

issues.description: getSupabaseClient() creates a new Supabase client on every invocation (every fetch, including every 10s poll). Other files in the codebase (todo-actions.ts, todo-board.tsx) cache the client in a module-level variable. While dashboard-client.tsx has the same non-caching pattern, this means a new GoTrueClient, RealtimeClient, etc. are instantiated every 10 seconds. Consider caching the client or reusing the pattern from todo-actions.ts. This is a pre-existing pattern inconsistency, not introduced by this feature alone.

The error state is cleared (setError(null)) at the start of every fetch, including background polls. If a poll fails after previously succeeding, the error is set, but the next poll attempt immediately clears it before trying again. This creates a brief flash where error disappears then potentially reappears. A minor UX consideration — not a bug, as the inline error banner handles stale-data-with-error correctly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Supabase query in logs/page.tsx chains .from().select().order() on consecutive lines rather than using intermediate variables. AGENTS.md mandates 'Every line of code should do exactly one thing' with intermediate variables. However, dashboard-client.tsx follows the exact same chaining pattern, suggesting this convention is not consistently enforced across the codebase. The convention documentation may need clarification about whether Supabase query builder chains are an acceptable exception.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: web/app/logs/page.tsx:61-64 chains .from('message_logs').select('*').order('created_at', { ascending: true }). Same pattern in web/components/dashboard-client.tsx:88-91.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Multiple client-side components define their own local getSupabaseClient() function with slightly different implementations (some cache, some don't). There is also web/lib/supabase.ts which exports a singleton client. AGENTS.md does not document which Supabase client creation pattern to use for client components. This leads to inconsistency: todo-actions.ts and todo-board.tsx cache the client, while dashboard-client.tsx and logs/page.tsx recreate it each time.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: web/lib/supabase.ts (singleton export), web/lib/todo-actions.ts:17 (cached), web/components/todo-board.tsx:25 (cached), web/components/dashboard-client.tsx:12 (not cached), web/app/logs/page.tsx:11 (not cached)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature specifies skillName 'frontend-worker' but this skill does not exist in .factory/skills/. The worker correctly noted this in skillFeedback.deviations and proceeded using mission-worker-base procedures and codebase patterns. The skill should either be created or the feature's skillName should be updated.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json has skillName: 'frontend-worker'. Worker transcript shows Skill invocation failed with 'Error: Skill frontend-worker not found'. .factory/skills/ directory does not exist.

summary: The web-logs-page feature passes review. All five requirements are fully implemented: (1) error handling displays user-visible messages with Try Again button instead of empty state, (2) 10-second auto-refresh polling with Live/Paused toggle and manual Refresh button, (3) inbound messages blue/left-aligned and outbound green/right-aligned, (4) messages load on initial mount, (5) agent-browser verified error state rendering. The implementation closely follows the established dashboard-client.tsx pattern. Two non-blocking issues noted: uncached Supabase client creation on every poll (consistent with dashboard-client but inconsistent with other files), and a minor UX consideration around error state clearing during polls. TypeScript and build both pass.
